topic: A close-up of a Newton's Cradle in motion on a desk, macro view.

parsed_generation.script:
[00:00-00:02] Shot 1: Macro close-up on the Newton's cradle on a desk, one sphere pulled back - Camera: Macro close-up, Framing: Close, Movement: Static, Subject: Newton's cradle.
[00:02-00:05] Shot 2: Macro close-up focusing on the center collision as momentum transfers - Camera: Macro close-up, Framing: Close, Movement: Static, Subject: Newton's cradle.
[00:05-00:08] Shot 3: Macro close-up of the opposite end swinging outward, energy returning - Camera: Macro close-up, Framing: Close, Movement: Static, Subject: Newton's cradle.
parsed_generation.narration: A macro close-up on the desk reveals the Newton's cradle, with one sphere pulled back, ready to swing. The second shot tightens on the center collision, showing momentum transfer through the row as the spheres kiss and click. In the final moment, the opposite end swings outward, energy traveling along the line. Together these macro close-ups frame a single subject on a desk that illustrates the core physics of momentum and energy transfer in a tangible, accessible way.

feedback:
The macro close-up focus on a Newton’s cradle on a desk aligns with the topic and the narration links actions to momentum, but the script omits an explicit opening action (like a ball being lifted) and lacks the varied shot types (wide and tracking) and consistent timing seen in the gold standard. To improve, start with a hand lifting the outer ball and releasing it, include a slow-motion center-impact and a wider shot showing rhythmic oscillation, and align timestamps and camera angles with a mix of macro, wide, and tracking shots. Add lighting and metal texture cues to enhance visual richness.
Failed checks: example_alignment
strengths: Clear macro close-ups that stay focused on the cradle
Narration ties visuals to momentum concepts
fixes: Introduce an opening action: hand lifting and releasing the outer ball
Incorporate wide or tracking shots to convey motion and rhythm
Standardize timestamps to a consistent cadence (e.g., [00:00-00:03], [00:03-00:05], [00:05-00:08]) and describe lighting/texture details
